technologies.name: AWS
technologies.evidence: The decision was driven by AWS’s comprehensive ecosystem of managed services, native support for infrastructure automation, and enterprise-grade security features.
technologies.reasoning: AWS is explicitly mentioned as the chosen cloud provider for the new venture due to its advantages over LiquidWeb.

technologies.name: Amazon EC2
technologies.evidence: The solution leverages a range of AWS services, including Amazon EC2, Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL, Amazon CloudFront, AWS Lambda, Amazon S3, and AWS Secrets Manager.
technologies.reasoning: Amazon EC2 is explicitly listed as part of the AWS services used in the solution.

technologies.name: Prometheus

technologies.reasoning: Prometheus is explicitly listed as part of the observability tools used.

technologies.name: Terraform
technologies.evidence: Entire infrastructure defined using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) with Terraform.
technologies.reasoning: Terraform is explicitly mentioned as used for infrastructure as code.

technologies.name: GitLab CI/CD
technologies.evidence: GitLab pipelines automate deployment of Terraform stacks on code merges, enabling true continuous deployment.
technologies.reasoning: GitLab CI/CD is explicitly mentioned as used for automation and deployment.

technologies.name: LiquidWeb
technologies.evidence: The customer’s existing products were hosted on LiquidWeb, which had served them adequately for simpler workloads.
technologies.reasoning: LiquidWeb is mentioned as the previous cloud provider.
